## How to handle ToU Version Update

The current solution to set the Version of "Terms of Use" for newly registered User is to get the Value from Environment
Variable named `TERMS_OF_USE_CURRENT_VERSION`
The Value is injected from GitHub Secret (same name: `TERMS_OF_USE_CURRENT_VERSION`) while deploying Backend.

Exactly this Value is shown as `"Terms of Use current Version"` in Frontend
on `Admin->Tools->Terms of Use Management`:
![devManuals_ToU1.png](data:image/png;base64,)

In order to deploy new Version of ToU follow this steps:

- Rename the file `TermsOfUseCurrent.md` into `TermsOfUseVx.y.md` (`Vx.y` is the version of ToU in this
  file), in order to have entire text for any of versions separately.
- Put the text of new ToU into the newly created file `TermsOfUseCurrent.md`.
- Before Deploying on target environment (`INT/TEST/PROD`) update the value of the secret for the corresponding
  environment:
  ![devManuals_ToU2.png](data:image/png;base64,)
- After the Deployment:
  - check the new current Value on `Admin->Tools->Terms of Use Management` in the text
    field `"Terms of Use current Version"`; all new Users will become from now on this new version;
  - perform Reset of Acceptance Flag and Update the Version of ToU for all available Users to force the Users to
    accept new ToU.
